page: Microsoft Azure D2_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor operating at 2.3 GHz. The instance is configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. It includes 8.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a resource density of 4.0 GB per core, and is bundled with 51 GB of local storage. There are no GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this server type.

In benchmark evaluations, the D2_v3 demonstrates performance metrics that consistently fall within the bottom 10% of tested cloud servers. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance score is 775.38, while the multi-core score is 921.29. Memory bandwidth tests show 70.93 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 8.65 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 6.17 GB/sec for large-block writes. Computational benchmarks such as Geekbench yield a single-core score of 778.0 and a multi-core score of 933.0, with software compilation scoring 1126.0 and ray tracing at 1209.0.

Database and application performance benchmarks reflect similar constraints, with Passmark database operations scoring 507.83 and Redis SET operations reaching 1.11 million operations per second. Static web serving throughput is measured at 1.32 GB/sec. For machine learning workloads, the server processes prompts at 42.97 tokens per second and generates text at 22.13 tokens per second using a small 135M parameter model. Given these low-tier performance metrics, this server is positioned as a low-cost entry point for lightweight workloads such as basic web serving, small-scale development environments, or simple background tasks that do not require high computational throughput or memory bandwidth.
description: Microsoft Azure D2_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with an x86_64 Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor clocked at 2.3 GHz, offering 2 dedicated vCPUs from 1 physical core with hyper-threading. The hardware configuration includes 8.0 GB of RAM, 51 GB of local storage, and no GPU. Across multiple benchmark suites, including stress-ng, Geekbench, and Passmark, the server consistently performs in the bottom 10% of tested instances. For example, it achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 778.0 and a multi-core score of 933.0, while Redis SET operations reach 1.11 million operations per second. Given its modest processing capabilities and low-tier memory bandwidth, the D2_v3 is qualitatively cost-efficient for lightweight workloads such as basic web serving, small development environments, and low-demand background tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D2_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs on an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 51 GB of local storage, suitable for lightweight workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D2_v3 is a general-purpose VM with 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and 51 GB local storage, designed for basic, low-demand workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly general-purpose virtual machine with dual vCPUs and local storage for lightweight development and basic web serving tasks.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB RAM
Bundled with 51 GB of local storage and no GPU accelerators
Low-tier benchmark performance falling within the bottom 10% of tested servers
Suitable for basic web serving, small development environments, and background tasks
Qualitatively cost-efficient for low-demand, non-compute-intensive workloads
